name: Five Points Bank
description_short: Five Points Bank is a Nebraska-based community bank serving central and eastern regions since 1971, offering personal, business, agricultural, and mortgage banking services.
description_long:
Five Points Bank has been operating as a community bank in Nebraska for 55 years, having been established in 1971. The bank serves customers across central and eastern Nebraska with multiple branch locations in cities including Omaha, Lincoln, Hastings, Grand Island, Kearney, Papillion LaVista, Elkhorn, and Sumner. The bank positions itself as a relationship-focused institution that emphasizes personalized service and deep community involvement.

Five Points Bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking products across three main customer segments: personal banking, business banking, and agriculture. Personal services include checking accounts, savings and money market accounts, certificates of deposit, IRA accounts, health savings accounts, credit cards, personal loans, and home mortgages. Business customers can access business checking, business savings, business certificates of deposit, business credit cards, and business lending services. The bank also provides specialized agricultural loans and cash management services for farm operations. Additional services include wealth management, trust services, investment management, safe deposit boxes, and financial planning resources.

Five Points Bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on local community presence and relationship banking. The bank employs relationship managers with extensive banking experience and actively participates in community events like shred days and parades. The bank recently welcomed three new relationship managers to its Lincoln team with combined 80+ years of banking experience. The website emphasizes that "smiling faces will greet you at the door" and positions the bank as more than just a financial institution—as "a family." The bank also maintains daily system maintenance windows (6-7 p.m. CST) to update customer transactions.

Five Points Bank is a legitimate, FDIC-insured institution backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. However, the bank serves a geographically limited market in Nebraska and may not be suitable for customers outside this region. The bank's website includes a fraud warning about sophisticated impersonation schemes in Nebraska, indicating awareness of security concerns. As a traditional community bank rather than a fintech or online-only lender, the service model relies on branch locations and relationship managers rather than digital-first experiences. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal savings and money market accounts
Certificates of deposit (CDs) for personal and business
Home mortgage lending (purchase, refinance, first-time homebuyer)
Business lending and cash management services
pros: FDIC-insured with full U.S. Government backing, providing deposit protection up to $250,000
55 years of established banking history in Nebraska communities since 1971
Multiple branch locations across central and eastern Nebraska for in-person service
Relationship managers with 80+ combined years of banking experience on staff
Comprehensive service offerings including personal, business, agricultural, mortgage, and wealth management products
Active community involvement and local engagement through sponsorships and events
Trust services and investment management available for wealth preservation
Agricultural lending specialized for farm operations, not just general lending
cons: Geographic limitation: serves only Nebraska communities; not accessible to customers in other states
Limited online functionality with daily maintenance windows (6-7 p.m. CST) reducing availability
Community bank model may offer fewer technological innovations compared to larger national or online-only banks
No mention of competitive rates or rate comparison information on website for deposits or loans
Branch-dependent service model means customers outside branch areas have limited access to in-person support

contact.address: 2015 N Broadwell Ave, Grand Island, NE 68803

best_for: Nebraska residents seeking relationship-based community banking with local branch access
Agricultural businesses and farm operations requiring specialized agricultural lending
Small to mid-size business owners needing business checking, lending, and cash management services
Customers prioritizing in-person service and community involvement over digital-first banking
diagnosis: Five Points Bank is best for Nebraska residents and businesses seeking relationship-driven community banking with physical branch access and personalized service. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—the bank only operates in Nebraska, making it inaccessible for customers outside this region, and its branch-dependent model may not appeal to customers seeking digital-first banking experiences.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 2015 N Broadwell Ave, Grand Island, NE 68803
meta_description: Five Points Bank offers personal, business, agricultural, and mortgage banking services to Nebraska communities with FDIC insurance.
